# Session Context: MPD Fork Packaging CI/CD Pipeline

**Date:** 2026-03-18  
**Handoff target:** Claude Code  
**Status:** Planning phase — no code generated yet

---

## Objective

Package a fork of MPD (Music Player Daemon) for distribution across all supported platforms, supporting both internal testing distribution (small team, no official repo submission) and a full release pipeline that mirrors official distributor workflows.

The project requires a CI/CD pipeline built on **GitHub Actions** that promotes builds through a structured branch topology and produces platform-native packaging artifacts for each target platform.

## Source Project

- **Upstream:** Music Player Daemon (MPD)
- **Repository:** [https://github.com/MusicPlayerDaemon/MPD](https://github.com/MusicPlayerDaemon/MPD)
- **Build system:** Meson
- **Language:** C++

## Target Platforms

| Platform | Package Format |
|---|---|
| Ubuntu / Debian | `.deb` |
| Fedora / RHEL | `.rpm` |
| Arch Linux | `PKGBUILD` / `.pkg.tar.zst` |
| macOS | Homebrew formula / `.pkg` |
| Windows | NSIS or WiX installer |

## Branch Topology

Each platform follows the same symmetric promotion pattern. Feature work is integrated first, then fans out to platform-specific lanes before converging at `main`.

```
feature|chore|fix
        │
        ▼
       dev
        │
        ▼
       int  ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │                                                  │
        ├──► test-ubuntu-debian ──► stage-ubuntu-debian ──┤
        │                                                  │
        ├──► test-fedora-rhel   ──► stage-fedora-rhel   ──┤
        │                                                  │
        ├──► test-arch          ──► stage-arch           ──┤
        │                                                  │
        ├──► test-macos         ──► stage-macos          ──┤
        │                                                  │
        └──► test-windows       ──► stage-windows        ──┤
                                                           │
                                                          main
```

**Note:** macOS and Windows follow the same symmetric promotion pattern. Full topology below.

## CI/CD Platform

**GitHub Actions** — confirmed.

### Runner Strategy by Platform

| Platform | Runner Approach |
|---|---|
| Ubuntu / Debian | GitHub-hosted `ubuntu-latest` |
| Fedora / RHEL | Docker container on `ubuntu-latest` host runner |
| macOS | GitHub-hosted `macos-latest` |
| Windows | GitHub-hosted `windows-latest` |
| Arch Linux | Docker container (`archlinux` image) on `ubuntu-latest` host runner |

## Resolved Decisions

| Decision | Resolution |
|---|---|
| Arch Linux runner | Option A — `archlinux` Docker container on `ubuntu-latest` host runner |
| Windows installer toolchain | WiX v4 |
| macOS and Windows branch topology | Confirmed symmetric — `test-macos` → `stage-macos`, `test-windows` → `stage-windows` |
| Signing infrastructure | Out of scope for this pipeline workstream — separate Feature |

## Open Questions

None blocking. All decisions required to begin domain modeling and workflow design are resolved.

## Constraints and Context

- Devin is the architect and implementer. No code should be generated by Claude Code until explicitly directed.
- Domain modeling is the preferred approach to architecture before implementation.
- Accuracy is prioritized over speed.
- The Meson build system must be respected — do not attempt to substitute CMake or autotools.
- All packaging must produce artifacts that work for both internal test distribution and eventual official-style release pipelines.

## Suggested Next Steps (Not Prescriptive)

1. Domain model — identify the core entities (Platform, Lane, Artifact, Runner, Job) and their relationships before any workflow YAML.
2. Design the Meson build and packaging matrix (per-platform build flags, packaging tool, artifact output).
3. Write workflow YAML starting from `int` → `test-*` lanes (internal distribution first).
4. Signing infrastructure addressed as a separate Feature before stage lanes are implemented.
